auth.setup_sub: Create your admin account to secure the dashboard
auth.setup_hint: This setup runs once. Your credentials are stored locally.

s.homey_host_hint: (Settings → General → IP Address in the Homey app)
s.unifi_apikey_hint: (preferred — from UniFi OS → Control Plane → API Keys)
s.sip_domain_hint: (leave blank to use server host from URL)
s.sip_dtmf_hint: (sent when you tap Unlock during a call)
s.sip_relay_hint: (pulse this relay on Unlock — leave blank for DTMF-only)
s.boneio_host_hint: (leave blank to reuse Cerbo GX broker)
s.le_staging: Use Let's Encrypt staging (for testing — won't issue trusted certs)
s.restart_sub: The server is restarting. Reconnecting in

graphs.empty: No numeric sensors reporting yet — history builds up while the server runs.

auto.scenes_empty: No scenes yet — create one-tap presets that set several devices at once.
auto.rules_empty: No rules yet — trigger actions or alerts when a sensor crosses a threshold.
auto.name: Name

s2.lsh_address_as_seen_by_the_miniserver: LSH address as seen by the Miniserver

sdesc.d0: Direct local connection to your Cerbo GX (recommended).
sdesc.d1: Used when local MQTT is unavailable.
sdesc.d2: Adds real-time solar production data from the SolarEdge Monitoring API. Runs alongside Victron data sources.
sdesc.d3: Adds SmartThings devices (switches, power meters, sensors) to the dashboard. Get a Personal Access Token from account.smartthings.com/tokens.
sdesc.d4: Connects to Athom Homey Pro 2023+ or Homey Cloud. All devices (lights, switches, sensors, thermostats, locks) are auto-discovered and appear in the dashboard with full control.
sdesc.d5: Connects to a Somfy TaHoma or Connexoon box on your local network. Roller shutters, screens, venetian blinds, awnings, gates, and garage doors are auto-discovered and controllable from the dashboard.
sdesc.d6: Controls Denon (and Marantz) AV receivers over the local network via the Telnet control protocol (port 23). Supports power on/standby, master volume, mute, and input selection.
sdesc.d7: Controls Sonos speakers on your local network via UPnP (port 1400). Auto-discovers all Zone Players via SSDP, or specify IPs manually. Provides play/pause, prev/next track, volume, and mute. No account required.
sdesc.d8: Controls AUX air conditioners via the AC Freedom cloud API. Supports on/off, temperature, mode (cool/heat/dry/fan/auto), and fan speed. Requires an AC Freedom account.
sdesc.d9: Connects to the Suppla cloud (cloud.supla.org) or a self-hosted Suppla server. Discovers all channels (switches, dimmers, thermometers, humidity sensors, shutters, gates) and groups them by device. Authentication uses a personal access token from the Suppla Cloud → Security → Personal Access Tokens page.
sdesc.d10: Connects to the Bayrol Pool Access cloud to read pH, ORP (redox), temperature, and free chlorine from a Pool Manager Connect device. Polling interval is 60 s by default.
sdesc.d11: Connects to Dreame robot vacuums and air purifiers locally via the Mi Home (miio) protocol. Each device needs its local IP and 32-char hex token — enable Developer mode in Mi Home, go to the device detail page, tap the three-dot menu → Share Device → 拷贝到剪贴板 to see the token, or use a third-party token extractor.
sdesc.d12: Connect to a Satel INTEGRA alarm panel via ETHM-1 Plus module (TCP port 7094). Zone sensors (violation + tamper), partition arm state (arm/disarm control), and programmable output relays appear as individual dashboard devices.
sdesc.d13: Auto-discovers cameras (shown in dashboard) and sensors (door, motion, temperature, humidity) from a UniFi Dream Machine or UNVR.
sdesc.d14: Connects to a Loxone Miniserver via WebSocket. All rooms, switches, dimmers, blinds, thermostats, and sensors are auto-discovered from the structure file.
sdesc.d15: Pushes sensor values from LSH to Loxone Virtual Inputs in real time (on change, debounced 200 ms). Configure one mapping per line: storeKey = VirtualInputName. Example: bayrol/19048/ph = PoolPH
sdesc.d16: Generates ready-to-import Virtual Output (commands) and Virtual HTTP Input (feedback) templates for Loxone Config 17.1 from your connected devices. Filter by brand, pick the API token to embed, and download — then import via Virtual Outputs / Virtual HTTP Inputs → template import in Loxone Config.
sdesc.d17: Connects to a KNXnet/IP gateway or IP router over TCP/IP. Group addresses are mapped to dashboard device cards with read/write support. Requires npm install knx.
sdesc.d18: Register as a SIP extension on UniFi Talk, Loxone, or any SIP PBX with WebSocket transport. Incoming calls (e.g. from a UniFi Access door reader) pop up with the matching camera feed and a one-tap unlock button.
sdesc.d19: Connects to your LG ThinQ account via the v1 cloud API. Use your LG account email and password to fetch tokens and user number automatically, or paste them manually. Password is never stored.
sdesc.d20: Connects to an IKEA Dirigera hub (2022+). Discovers lights, outlets, blinds, motion sensors, and VINDSTYRKA air quality sensors. Requires a bearer token obtained once via node scripts/dirigera-auth.js <hub-ip> — press the button on the hub when prompted.
sdesc.d21: Connects to an older IKEA Tradfri gateway via CoAP/DTLS. Requires npm install node-tradfri-client. First run: enter the security code from the sticker on the gateway — generated identity and PSK are printed to the server log. Subsequent runs: leave the code blank and fill in identity + PSK.
sdesc.d22: Connects to a BoneIO controller via MQTT. Relays, inputs, and sensors are auto-discovered through Home Assistant MQTT discovery. Leave host blank to use the Cerbo GX MQTT broker.
sdesc.d23: Connects to SmartBob via MQTT. Add the broker address and define each entity with its state topic, optional command topic, and type.
sdesc.d24: Subscribe to any MQTT topic and map JSON fields to sensor readings or controllable switches. Works with Arduino, ESP32, Tasmota custom firmware, or any device publishing JSON over MQTT.
sdesc.d25: Connects to ESP32/ESP8266 devices running ESPHome with the web_server: component enabled. Sensors, switches, binary sensors, lights, and covers are auto-discovered via the device event stream.
sdesc.d26: Add Shelly Gen1 and Gen2 smart devices by IP address. Switches, dimmers, plugs, and sensors are auto-detected and polled every 30 s.
sdesc.d27: Control BroadLink RM4 Pro / RM4 Mini IR blasters and RF radio transmitters. Learn IR and RF codes by name, store them locally, and trigger them from the dashboard or via API. MAC address is printed on the device label.
sdesc.d28: Connects to a Fibaro Home Center 2 or Home Center 3 (Lite) via its local REST API. Devices are grouped by room into cards on the dashboard. Switches, dimmers, roller shutters, and sensors are discovered automatically.
sdesc.d29: Control Waveshare relay boards over Modbus TCP (port 502). Supports any Waveshare relay module with Ethernet or a serial-to-Ethernet converter. Each board appears as a device card with individual relay toggles.
sdesc.d30: Add an Aeotec Indoor Camera 360° by local IP. Sensor data (motion, sound) flows via SmartThings; the local RTSP stream is added to your Cameras list for live video. Credentials are the ones you set in the Aeotec app.
sdesc.d31: Reolink PoE cameras and NVRs. One row per camera — use channel 0 for a standalone camera, or add a row per NVR channel. Snapshots are proxied so the password stays server-side.
sdesc.d32: Cameras shown on the dashboard. Provide an RTSP URL and optionally a snapshot URL (HTTP JPEG endpoint) for live preview.
sdesc.d33: Re-streams each camera's RTSP URL through a built-in RTSP server so Loxone (or any RTSP client) can connect to a single clean URL. Requires ffmpeg installed on the server. Each camera gets a dedicated port starting at Base Port.
sdesc.d34: Name the relays on your Cerbo GX. Leave empty to hide the relay card from the dashboard entirely.
sdesc.d35: Pair the relay bridge in Apple Home using the PIN below.
sdesc.d36: Manage users, API tokens, and HTTPS/TLS encryption.
sdesc.d37: Save your entire configuration to a file or restore from a previous backup.
